title: elaeocarpus ngii subsp. curtisii
intro: Elaeocarpus ngii subsp. curtisii, also known as the Fraser's Elm, is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpaceae family, native to Malaysia. The Fraser's Elm has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits include treating fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
